commit.completeDescription: The git commit command captures a snapshot of the project's currently staged changes. It allows you to save your work and create a history of changes in the repository.

-m.completeDescription: Sets the commit message for the commit

-a.description: Automatically stage files that have been modified
-a.completeDescription: Automatically stages all modified files before committing

--amend.description: Replace the tip of the current branch by creating a new commit
--amend.completeDescription: Allows you to modify the most recent commit

checkout.description: Switch branches or restore working tree files
checkout.completeDescription: Switches between branches or restores working tree files

-b.description: Create and checkout a new branch
-b.completeDescription: Creates a new branch and switches to it

push.description: Update remote refs along with associated objects
push.completeDescription: Updates remote references with the local changes
